argon2 (hash-wasm) — vendored
==============================

Upstream:   https://github.com/Daninet/hash-wasm
Package:    hash-wasm (per-algorithm Argon2 bundle, dist/argon2.umd.min.js)
License:    MIT

Status
------
VENDORED — hash-wasm@4.12.0 (see VERSION). Pulled + SHA-verified by
scripts/vendor-argon2.sh (EXPECTED_DIST_SHA256 is pinned there). The
pinned hash was additionally cross-checked against the npm registry
tarball (registry.npmjs.org/hash-wasm/-/hash-wasm-4.12.0.tgz →
package/dist/argon2.umd.min.js) on 2026-06-12 — CDN and registry bytes
are identical. Re-run that script to bump; do not pull "@latest".

What's here
-----------
  - argon2.js          (the npm dist/argon2.umd.min.js verbatim, PLUS an
                        appended ESM adapter: the upstream dist is
                        UMD-only, and in ES-module scope its factory
                        takes the globalThis branch — the append
                        re-exports `argon2id` from the global it
                        populated and adds the `VENDORED` sentinel.
                        The appended lines are the only modification to
                        the upstream bytes.)
  - argon2.js.SHA256   (the UPSTREAM dist hash, pre-append — tamper floor)
  - VERSION            (the pinned npm release the bytes came from)

Why this implementation (and not antelle/argon2-browser)
--------------------------------------------------------
Both candidates are MIT. hash-wasm won on fit for the MV3 service
worker:

  - Single self-contained ~29 KB file: the Argon2 WASM binary (a small,
    audited, hand-tuned C compile) is base64-embedded in the JS, so
    there is no separate .wasm asset to locate/fetch — no
    URL-resolution or instantiateStreaming path-sniffing to patch for
    the SW context. antelle/argon2-browser's loader sniffs its
    environment (document/process/importScripts) and resolves the
    .wasm by URL, which needs patching to run cleanly as an extension
    ES module.
  - No DOM, no SharedArrayBuffer, no worker spawning: hash-wasm's
    argon2id runs the lanes sequentially in-process. peerd uses
    parallelism=1 anyway (single-lane, lean on memory), so the missing
    thread pool costs nothing.
  - Per-algorithm bundle: we vendor ONLY Argon2 (+ its internal
    BLAKE2b dependency, which Argon2's H0/H' functions require), not
    the whole multi-hash library.
  - Actively maintained, widely used (~1M weekly downloads), with an
    extensive test-vector suite (RFC 9106 vectors included upstream).

Why vendored, not lazy-imported from CDN
----------------------------------------
MV3 CSP forbids remote script execution
(script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'). The Argon2 implementation
derives the vault's key-encryption key from the user's passphrase —
the most security-sensitive code path in the extension — so it must be
pinned, hash-verified bytes in the extension package, never a network
fetch. `wasm-unsafe-eval` (already in the manifest CSP for CheerpX/
Moonshine) is what allows WebAssembly.instantiate of the embedded
binary inside the service worker.

Where it's used
---------------
extension/peerd-egress/vault/argon2.js — the thin wrapper that adapts
hash-wasm's option names to the vault's KDF descriptor shape
({ memKiB, iters, parallelism, salt }). The vault core never imports
this file directly; the wrapper is injected as the `argon2` dep by the
service worker (functional core / imperative shell).

Update procedure
----------------
1. Edit scripts/vendor-argon2.sh to bump HASH_WASM_VERSION.
2. Pull the registry tarball for the new version, extract
   package/dist/argon2.umd.min.js, shasum -a 256 it, and set
   EXPECTED_DIST_SHA256 to that value (registry and CDN must agree).
3. Run the script. It downloads, verifies, re-appends the ESM adapter,
   and rewrites argon2.js.SHA256 + VERSION.
4. Re-run the vault test suites (bun + in-browser) and update this
   file's Status block with the new version + date.
